This is genuinely clever. The parallax-through-a-window effect is something I have seen done in mobile wallpapers but rarely pulled off well on desktop. Have you considered using facial landmark detection instead of just head position? It might give you smoother tracking since you would have more stable reference points to work with.

This is genuinely one of the coolest side projects I've seen on here. The parallax effect using head tracking is such a clever use of the front camera. Would love to see how it handles low light conditions — that's usually where face tracking gets shaky. Excited for the beta!

positive.comment: It tracks your head position with the webcam and adjusts the camera of a small 3D scene accordingly. So when you move left/right the perspective shifts, which creates the depth illusion. I explained it a bit more here:
positive.parent: How does it work tho?

positive.comment: Yes, the demo is a small 3D scene and the camera moves based on your head position.
positive.parent: Is it a 3D scene?

positive.comment: Totally fair concern. All the head tracking runs locally on the computer. No video is stored or sent anywhere. The webcam is only used to estimate the head position for the perspective effect.
positive.parent: This is badass. I however do not think many people want their video streaming into a random app unless it's open source / guaranteed to be secure.

positive.comment: Good question! I actually answered this in the FAQ on the landing page. Short version: the perspective can only be correct for one viewpoint at a time, so it tracks a single viewer. More details here:
positive.parent: What if two person are staring at the screen, who will it track?

positive.comment: Good point. Right now you can't do a call and run the wallpaper at the same time. I'm working on making it automatically stop using the camera when another app (like a meeting app) needs it.
positive.parent: Cool idea! Wouldn't it block the use of the camera for meetings or other purposes while the wallpaper is running?

positive.comment: Good question! I actually put together a page where I replied to most of the common questions since a lot of them were similar.
positive.parent: It is cool, but how much of the computer's resources does it take to keep it as a wallpaper?

positive.comment: It's basically head-tracking with the webcam + perspective rendering so the scene shifts based on where your head is.
positive.parent: How did you do this? This is insane

positive.comment: Right now it tracks a single face, so if two people move in opposite directions it will usually follow whichever face gets detected first.
positive.parent: What happens when 2 people look at it and go opposite sides at the same time?

positive.comment: Fair point , The head tracking runs locally and no video is stored or sent anywhere it only estimates the head position for the effect
